channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Columbia University activist Mahmoud Khalil can be deported, immigration judge finds
item.description: Columbia University graduate student Mahmoud Khalil can be kicked out of the U.S. as a national security risk, an immigration judge in Louisiana found Friday during a hearing over the legality of deporting the activist who participated in pro-Palestinian demonstrations.
item.dc:creator: SARA CLINE and KATE BRUMBACK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: From classifying immigrants as dead to deportation: A guide to actions on Trump immigration policies
item.description: President Donald Trump's immigration agenda is playing out in numerous ways Friday, from hearings in key cases on the government's power to deport people to the start of a registry required for all those who are in the country illegally.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: US may already be in recession: BlackRock CEO
item.description: BlackRock CEO Larry Fink said that the United States may already be in a recession or getting very close to one.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: South Carolina executes second man by firing squad in 5 weeks
item.description: A firing squad on Friday executed a South Carolina man who killed an off-duty police officer, the second time the rare execution method has been used by the state in the past five weeks.
item.dc:creator: Jeffrey Collins, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Divers search for parts after NYC helicopter crash killed a family of five and the pilot, a Navy vet
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Divers using sonar searched Friday for key pieces of a sightseeing helicopter that broke apart in midair and plunged into the Hudson River between Manhattan and New Jersey. All six people aboard were killed — a family of five from Spain and the pilot, a 36-year-old U.S. Navy veteran.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, DAVE COLLINS and TED SHAFFREY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Universal announces UK theme park amid 'epic' growth of parks business
item.description: Universal Destinations & Experiences is expanding its global theme park footprint, confirming a new theme park will be built in the U.K. -- the latest in a trio of ambitious projects for the company.
item.dc:creator: Scott Gustin, Fox 5

item.title: Florida universities to deputize campus police for immigration enforcement
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (AP) — The police departments of at least three public universities in Florida are seeking agreements with the federal government to carry out immigration enforcement on campus.
item.dc:creator: KATE PAYNE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Justice Department tasked with enforcing Trump's transgender sports ban in Maine
item.description: PORTLAND, Maine (AP) — Maine officials said Friday the state will not comply with a ban on transgender athletes in high school sports in the wake of a Trump administration finding that the state violated antidiscrimination laws by allowing the students to participate. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Immigration judge allows Mahmoud Khalil deportation effort to proceed
item.description: A judge in former Columbia University activist Mahmoud Khalil's case ruled against him Friday, finding he is eligible for deportation while giving his legal team a little less than two weeks to respond.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WGN 9

item.title: Trump administration adds immigrant Social Security numbers to 'death list' in deportation push
item.description: The Trump administration directed the Social Security Administration (SSA) to place more than 6,300 migrants on its list for tracking dead people, blocking their ability to work in a move it hopes will encourage them to “self-deport.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump administration refers Maine to DOJ over transgender athletes
item.description: The Department of Education on Friday referred a Title IX investigation into Maine schools to the Justice Department after the state failed to reach a resolution with the Trump administration over a finding that it violated federal anti-discrimination law by allowing transgender students to particip
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Amazon CEO: Sellers will try to pass tariff costs along to buyers
item.description: Amazon’s network includes millions of third-party sellers who are either in China or whose products originate from there.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Russian American ballerina Ksenia Karelina has message for Trump after release from Russia
item.description: Russian-American ballerina Ksenia Karelina said she was "so grateful" to President Donald Trump after arriving in the U.S. following a prisoner swap with Russia.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: No, Casey Anthony did not open a Branson daycare
item.description: Google Trends showed a variety of topics being searched in Missouri on Thursday morning---sports, weather, the lottery, and one specific question: Did Casey Anthony open a daycare in Branson, Missouri?
item.dc:creator: Megan Mueller, Fox 5

item.title: The Latest: Trump administration offers no details on mistakenly deported man after court ruling
item.description: A federal judge lambasted a government lawyer who couldn’t explain what, if anything, President Donald Trump’s administration has done to arrange for the return of a Maryland man who was mistakenly deported last month to a notorious prison in El Salvador.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Supreme Court rules Trump administration must 'facilitate’ return of mistakenly deported man
item.description: The court’s emergency ruling instructs the lower judge to clarify the language of her order but said she acted properly in ensuring that Kilmar Abrego Garcia’s case is handled “as it would have been had he not been improperly sent to El Salvador.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, WGN 9

item.title: Connecticut house of horrors stepmom denies child abuse accusations that came as ‘extreme shock’: attorney
item.description: Kimberly Sullivan, the Waterbury, Connecticut woman accused of abusing her stepson, denies the accusations that came as an "extreme shock," her attorney said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: ICE highlights arrests of 5 illegal aliens convicted of violent crimes, including against children
item.description: U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement highlighted five arrests made by agents across the United States on Wednesday. All five people are convicted criminals and Mexican nationals.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pro-Palestinian demonstrators who occupied Stanford building are charged with felonies
item.description: SAN JOSE, Calif. (AP) — A dozen pro-Palestinian demonstrators who were arrested at Stanford University last year after they occupied and allegedly caused hundreds of thousands of dollars in damage to a campus building are now facing charges.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Masked anti-Israel Stanford protesters charged in destructive 2024 campus building takeover
item.description: A dozen pro-Palestinian protesters charged in connection to the June 2024 takeover and destruction of a building at Stanford University.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Here's how to use Zelle now that app has changed
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (WFLA) — Zelle has announced a big change. Its standalone app will be used for only one thing: to educate consumers about scams and fraud. The person-to-person transfers that made Zelle popular are still available, but only if your bank belongs to the Zelle network.
item.dc:creator: Shannon Behnken, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US government moves for release of ex-FBI informant who fabricated bribery story about the Bidens
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — The U.S. government now wants a former FBI informant who fabricated a story about former President Joe Biden and his son Hunter accepting bribes to be released from prison while he appeals his conviction, it was revealed Thursday in court records.
item.dc:creator: RIO YAMAT, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Near record heat builds across Utah ahead of cooldown
item.description: The heat is on across the Beehive State for the next few days with cooler weather forecasted to set in Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Alana Brophy, ABC 4

item.title: Vietnam veteran finds PTSD relief with RTM therapy
item.description: RTM therapy is gaining popularity as a PTSD treatment, with a reportedly high success rate and little risk of relapse.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, NewsNation

item.title: Witness captures video of helicopter breaking apart, crashing into Hudson River
item.description: A witness captured video of the moment a helicopter came crashing down into the Hudson River between Manhattan and New Jersey Thursday, killing six people.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Wisconsin Democratic leader who revitalized state party in swing state is leaving
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — The Wisconsin Democratic leader who revitalized the party in the presidential swing state announced Thursday he will be leaving his position in June after a six-year run that included a string of victories and “agonizingly close” losses.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT BAUER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Hip-hop exec, accused LA gang leader pleads not guilty to racketeering, extortion charges
item.description: A music executive who authorities allege is a leader of a notorious Los Angeles street gang appeared in court Tuesday to face a wide range of charges, including extortion, racketeering and conspiracy.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, WGN 9

item.title: Trump nominee for public lands post withdraws after her criticism of Jan. 6 attack surfaces
item.description: BILLINGS, Mont. (AP) — President Donald Trump's nominee to oversee an agency that manages a quarter-billion acres of public land has withdrawn her nomination following revelations that she criticized the Republican president in 2021 for inciting the Jan. 6 attack on the U.S. Capitol.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW BROWN and MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump orders agencies to ‘sunset’ environmental protections
item.description: The order applies to all regulations issued under laws governing things like energy appliance standards, mining and offshore drilling — as well as regulations issued under the Endangered Species Act.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, KRON4

item.title: Hearing looks at rules for evidence and other details in Bryan Kohberger's quadruple murder trial
item.description: BOISE, Idaho (AP) — Prosecutors and attorneys for a man charged in the killings of four University of Idaho students in 2022 argued some of the final ground rules they want for Bryan Kohberger's trial in a hearing Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA BOONE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Republican bill requiring proof of citizenship for voting passes US House
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The House passed one of Republicans' signature issues for the year on Thursday, approving legislation to require proof of U.S. citizenship when registering to vote for federal elections, one of President Donald Trump's top election-related priorities.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINA A. CASSIDY, CHRISTINE FERNANDO and LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Hundreds of students, dozens of colleges hit by Trump's visa purge: What to know
item.description: Hundreds of foreign students at dozens of colleges across the country have had their higher education experience turned upside down as the Trump administration has expanded its immigration crackdown beyond those involved in the pro-Palestinian protests.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KRON4

item.title: Homeland Security agents denied entry at two Los Angeles schools
item.description: It is believed to be the first time federal agents tried to enter Los Angeles schools since President Trump announced a crackdown on immigration.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KRON4

item.title: UTA announces 'significant' changes to several bus services including new routes
item.description: The Utah Transit Authority said significant changes are coming to its services in Davis, Salt Lake, and Utah County.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, ABC 4

item.title: S&P 500 sinks 3.5% as Wall Street swings from euphoria back to worries about US-China trade war
item.description: The losses for U.S. stocks accelerated Thursday after the White House clarified that Chinese imports will be tariffed at 145%, not the 125% rate that Trump had written about in his posting on Truth Social Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Author Jillian Lauren, wife of Weezer bassist, arrested after being shot by police
item.description: Jillian Lauren, author and wife of Weezer bassist Scott Shriner, was shot and injured by Los Angeles police and arrested on suspicion of attempted murder after pointing a gun at officers from her front yard, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Russian-American ballerina from Los Angeles released from Russia
item.description: A U.S.-Russian dual national imprisoned in Russia on treason charges was freed Thursday in exchange for a Russian man jailed on smuggling charges in the U.S., a prisoner swap that comes as Moscow and Washington have made efforts to repair ties.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5
